office_does: The sheriff preserves the peace, makes arrests, executes court process, and operates the county jail.
race_blurb: Three-term incumbent Ray Maycumber faces former Ferry County deputy and longtime Border Patrol agent Sam Olson.

candidates.summary: Maycumber is a three-term incumbent sheriff with more than 25 years as a Ferry County peace officer who emphasizes staffing and local authority over sheriff qualifications.
highlights: Has served as sheriff since 2015 after prior police, deputy, chief-deputy, and 911 roles [S1].
Identifies recruitment and retention as a major challenge [S1].
Opposes state legislation changing sheriff qualification and retention rules [S1][S2].

experience.basis: Three terms as sheriff and more than 25 years in local law enforcement establish a maximal proven record.

candidates.summary: Olson is a former Ferry County deputy and Republic officer with 20 years in the U.S. Border Patrol who proposes aggressive enforcement against theft and drug abuse.
highlights: Has local patrol and narcotics experience plus 20 years in the Border Patrol [S1].
Prioritizes household and property security and aggressive action against theft and drug abuse [S1].

safety.basis: His explicit operational priority is aggressive enforcement against theft and drug abuse.

experience.basis: Local patrol and narcotics work plus 20 years of federal law-enforcement service establish a maximal proven record.
